title: mk-cli --from-md1 stub uses encoding-sensitive Md1EncodingId hash, not the toolkit's encoding-stable WalletPolicyId — cross-repo linkage breaks
location: crates/mk-cli/src/cmd/mod.rs:57-63 (derive_stub_from_md1); used at crates/mk-cli/src/cmd/encode.rs:68-70 and crates/mk-cli/src/cmd/verify.rs:106-117. Toolkit counterpart: crates/mnemonic-toolkit/src/synthesize.rs:157-159 (stamp) and crates/mnemonic-toolkit/src/cmd/bundle.rs:2078-2096 (self-check)
claim: mk-cli's derive_stub_from_md1 computes the 4-byte policy_id_stub as SHA-256(md_codec::encode_payload(descriptor))[..4], which is byte-identical to md-codec's Md1EncodingId[..4] (encoding-sensitive: header/version, path-decl, TLV ordering all hashed). The toolkit stamps and validates mk1 cards with compute_wallet_policy_id(descriptor).as_bytes()[..4] = WalletPolicyId[..4], a structurally different, encoding-stable preimage. The two 4-byte stubs differ for the same descriptor, so `mk verify --from-md1` against a real toolkit card reports ContentMismatch on policy_id_stubs, and `mk encode --from-md1` produces cards the toolkit's bundle --self-check rejects.
evidence: mod.rs:58-61: decode_md1_string -> encode_payload -> sha256 -> [..4]. md-codec identity.rs:39-44 compute_md1_encoding_id is the identical hash (SHA-256(encode_payload)[0..16]) so mk-cli stub == Md1EncodingId[..4]. encode.rs:69 and verify.rs:107 both push derive_stub_from_md1(md1). Toolkit synthesize.rs:157 `let policy_id = md_codec::compute_wallet_policy_id(&descriptor)` then :159 `stub.copy_from_slice(&policy_id.as_bytes()[..4])` (also at 189/191, 242/244, 422/424, 594/596). Toolkit cmd/bundle.rs:2078-2083 recomputes `compute_wallet_policy_id(&desc).as_bytes()[..4]` as expected_stub and :2092-2096 rejects with 'mk1 policy_id_stubs do not include descriptor's stub' if absent. The two SHA-256 preimages are structurally distinct: encode_payload (md-codec encode.rs:79-91) writes header+path_decl+use_site_path+tree+full TLV section, whereas compute_wallet_policy_id (identity.rs:172-240) hashes canonical_template_tree_bytes || per-@N records with a custom presence_byte framing — so the stubs differ for essentially every descriptor. md-codec lib.rs:46,50-51 re-exports BOTH encode_payload AND compute_wallet_policy_id/WalletPolicyId, so the canonical function was available. mk SPEC_mk_v0_1.md:186,312 defines the stub as SHA-256(canonical_bytecode)[0..16] top 4 (== the legacy Md1EncodingId semantics) and predates md-codec's WalletPolicyId; SPEC line 328 even notes mk1 impls 'SHOULD use that helper directly rather than reimplementing the SHA-256 construction'.
suggested_fix: Change derive_stub_from_md1 to `let pid = md_codec::compute_wallet_policy_id(&descriptor)?; pid.as_bytes()[..4]` so the mk CLI's stub matches the toolkit's authoritative WalletPolicyId-based stub. NOTE a cross-version caveat the fix must clear: mk-cli pins md-codec = "0.34.0" (Cargo.lock) while the toolkit links md-codec 0.35.0 — confirm 0.34.0 both exposes compute_wallet_policy_id and produces byte-identical WalletPolicyId output to 0.35.0, else bump mk-cli's md-codec pin in lockstep. Update mk SPEC §3.3/§5 to reference WalletPolicyId. File companion FOLLOWUPs in both repos so the linkage is gate-tested cross-repo.
verification_note: Independently confirmed at source: mk-cli mod.rs:60-61 hashes encode_payload bytes = identity.rs:41 compute_md1_encoding_id hash. Toolkit synthesize.rs:157-159 stamps WalletPolicyId[..4] and cmd/bundle.rs:2078-2096 self-check rejects any mk1 card lacking that exact stub. Verified the two preimages are structurally different (encode.rs:79-91 full payload vs identity.rs:172-240 tree+records), so the stubs diverge in practice, not just theory. Corrected the auditor's toolkit path (it is crates/mnemonic-toolkit/src/cmd/bundle.rs, not src/bundle.rs; line numbers 2078-2096 match). Severity held at important NOT critical: the policy_id_stub is documented (SPEC §5 step 2) as an indexing aid / fast filter, not the cryptographic anchor (that is the Wallet Instance ID, step 4); the divergence is confined to mk-cli's standalone --from-md1 path (toolkit never calls derive_stub_from_md1 nor uses encode_payload for stubs — grep clean), so it is a real cross-CLI contract break, not funds loss or silent wrong output.

title: decode_path accepts both standard-table and explicit-0xFE encodings of the same path (two valid wire forms per KeyCard)
claim: A standard-table path (e.g. m/44'/0'/0') can be decoded either from its 1-byte table indicator or from an explicit 0xFE+count+LEB128 encoding; decode_path accepts both and yields the same DerivationPath. The encoder always emits the table form, so a hand-crafted explicit-form bytecode decodes to an identical KeyCard — mk1 wire is non-canonical at the path layer (decode is many-to-one). No wrong output / funds loss today; matters only if mk1 bytes are ever hashed for identity.
evidence: path.rs:103-104 takes the EXPLICIT_PATH_INDICATOR (0xFE) branch unconditionally when present; :106-108 otherwise does a table-indicator lookup; both return a valid path with no rejection of an explicit-form encoding that collides with a table entry. encode_path:86-88 always returns the single table indicator byte when lookup_path succeeds, only falling through to the 0xFE escape at :90-97 for non-table paths. A decode->re-encode round-trip therefore normalizes any explicit-form collision back to the table form.
suggested_fix: Either document explicitly that mk1 wire is non-canonical at the path layer (decode is many-to-one), or have decode_path reject an explicit-form encoding whose resolved path matches a standard-table entry (forcing canonical form). Low priority unless mk1 bytes are ever hashed for identity.
verification_note: Confirmed at path.rs:103-109: decode accepts both 0xFE-explicit and table-indicator forms with no collision rejection; encode_path:86-88 always prefers the table indicator. Verified the cross-chunk integrity hash (chunk.rs:67) is over canonical_bytecode produced only by the canonical encoder, and a decode->re-encode normalizes, so the non-canonical form is genuinely latent — never active in production. Severity held at observation: no wrong output, no funds risk; pure latent-canonicity note.

title: StringLayerHeader::to_5bit_symbols does total_chunks - 1 with no guard against total_chunks == 0 (unreachable today)
claim: `let total_chunks_wire = (total_chunks - 1) & 0x1F;` underflows if total_chunks == 0 (panic in debug, wrap to 255 then &0x1F=31 in release). The only production constructor of Chunked headers is split_into_chunks, which sets total = stream.len().div_ceil(frag_size).max(1), so total_chunks is always >= 1 and this is not externally reachable. Robustness gap because the encode-side invariant is implicit and a future direct constructor of the pub Chunked variant could trip it.
evidence: header.rs:88 subtracts 1 from a u8 unconditionally with no debug_assert. The StringLayerHeader::Chunked variant is pub (header.rs:45-56) and constructible by any in-crate caller. chunk.rs:73 `let total: usize = stream.len().div_ceil(frag_size).max(1)` is the sole guarantor of >= 1 in production (and stream.len() >= 4 from the appended cross-chunk hash bytes, so div_ceil already yields >=1). The decode side has an analogous defensive check at header.rs:154; the encode side at :88 has none.
suggested_fix: Add `debug_assert!(total_chunks >= 1)` at header.rs:88 (mirroring the decode-side check at :154) so the encode-side invariant is explicit and a future direct constructor fails loudly.
verification_note: Confirmed at header.rs:88 the unconditional u8 subtraction with no guard, and chunk.rs:73's .max(1) as the only production guarantor (Chunked variant is pub at header.rs:45). Verified the underflow is unreachable today via split_into_chunks. Severity held at observation: latent robustness/defensiveness gap with no current reachable defect.
